title: Sleep! Sleep! Beauty Bright
content:
Sleep! sleep! beauty bright,
    Dreaming o'er the joys of night;
    Sleep! sleep! in thy sleep
    Little sorrows sit and weep.

    Sweet Babe, in thy face
    Soft desires I can trace,
    Secret joys and secret smiles,
    Little pretty infant wiles.

    As thy softest limbs I feel,
    Smiles as of the morning steal
    O'er thy cheek, and o'er thy breast
    Where thy little heart does rest.

    O! the cunning wiles that creep
    In thy little heart asleep.
    When thy little heart does wake
    Then the dreadful lightnings break,

    From thy cheek and from thy eye,
    O'er the youthful harvests nigh.
    Infant wiles and infant smiles
    Heaven and Earth of peace beguiles.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Blake, titled "Sleep! Sleep! Beauty Bright", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
